title: cerastium vulgatum subsp. fontanum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum subsp. fontanum, commonly known as the field chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cerastium vulgatum subsp. fontanum include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive problems. Cerastium vulgatum subsp. fontanum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
